name: Loop quantum cosmology
summary: Applies loop-quantum-gravity quantisation techniques to homogeneous-isotropic cosmological minisuperspaces. The discrete-area structure of LQG produces a quantum-bounce replacement of the Big Bang singularity: contracting universes rebound at densities of order the Planck scale and become expanding ones. Predicts modifications to the primordial power spectrum at long wavelengths and a specific bounce density.

addresses_puzzles.note: Quantum-bounce replaces the singular initial state with a smooth pre-Big-Bang phase, providing an alternative to inflationary initial conditions. Specific predictions for CMB low-multipole power and B-mode features distinguish LQC from inflation-only scenarios.
Symmetry-reduced LQG applied to FRW cosmology; resolves the Big Bang singularity via a quantum bounce but is not a complete QG framework by itself (Bojowald 2001; Ashtekar-Pawlowski-Singh 2006).

touches_benchmarks.note: Pre-bounce contracting phase modifies the primordial power spectrum at the largest observable scales; could account for some low-multipole anomalies but predictions are model-dependent.

introduces.note: Bounce density (set by LQG area gap) and Barbero-Immirzi parameter.

history: Bojowald (2001), 'Absence of singularity in loop quantum cosmology', Phys. Rev. Lett. 86, 5227, arXiv:gr-qc/0102069. Comprehensive review: Ashtekar-Singh (2011), 'Loop quantum cosmology: a status report', Class. Quantum Grav. 28, 213001, arXiv:1108.0893. Active small-community research with steady progress on bounce phenomenology.

references.citation: Bojowald, 'Absence of singularity in loop quantum cosmology', Phys. Rev. Lett. 86, 5227 (2001)
Ashtekar and Singh, 'Loop quantum cosmology: a status report', Class. Quantum Grav. 28, 213001 (2011)
